title: I Built Myself a House of Glass by Edward Thomas — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
I BUILT myself a house of glass:
It took me years to make it:
And I was proud. But now, alas,
Would God someone would break it.
But it looks too magnificent.
No neighbour casts a stone
From where he dwells, in tenement
Or palace of glass, alone.
meaning: "I Built Myself a House of Glass" by Edward Thomas is a deep poem consisting of 8 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "I BUILT myself a house of glass: It took me years to make it:...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Thomas's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
